hypothesis: If an LLM agent equipped with iterative feedback and domain-specific external tools (memory, periodic table, knowledge base) is used to guide compositional proposals for inorganic materials, then it will achieve higher success rates in targeting specific formation energies with greater compositional validity, uniqueness, and novelty compared to tool-free LLM variants and purely generative diffusion models, because the structured retrieval of chemical knowledge and iterative self-refinement enable the agent to systematically navigate the materials composition space more effectively than unguided generation.
